canonical_definition: Vicuna-Bench is a benchmark commonly used to evaluate the performance of Large Language Models, with a prevailing focus on conversational and instruction-following abilities. Across the provided literature, a recurring feature of its operationalization is the use of an automated judge, most frequently identified as GPT-4, to assess model responses to user prompts. One paper specifies that this evaluation involves comparing a model's output against a reference model such as gpt-3.5-turbo. The benchmark's data is described as consisting of user prompts, with sources mentioning "user-shared conversations" or a specific set of "80 single-turn chat prompts." While some definitions frame its purpose broadly, the more specific and common descriptions state it measures "chatbot capability" and "response quality on short-text, conversational tasks." The provided work also shows Vicuna-Bench being used to measure constructs such as `Commonsense knowledge`, `Evaluation`, and `Text generation`. In practice, it is frequently employed alongside other evaluation tools, with several papers mentioning it in conjunction with MT-Bench and AlpacaEval. The benchmark is also referred to as VicunaEval in some contexts.

definitions.text: A benchmark for assessing LLM responses to user prompts, used to evaluate the correlation between evaluator models and human or GPT-4 scores.
definitions.paper: Prometheus: Inducing Fine-Grained Evaluation Capability in Language Models

definitions.text: Chatbot benchmark using user-shared conversations evaluated by GPT-4 to assess model performance in open-ended dialogue.
definitions.paper: SALMON: Self-Alignment with Instructable Reward Models

definitions.text: A benchmark used to evaluate a model's instruction-following and conversational abilities, where model outputs are compared against a reference model (gpt-3.5-turbo) using GPT-4 as the judge.
definitions.paper: OpenChat: Advancing Open-source Language Models with Mixed-Quality Data

definitions.text: A benchmark used to evaluate the performance of LLMs on a variety of tasks.
definitions.paper: SeRA: Self-Reviewing and Alignment of LLMs using Implicit Reward Margins

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset used to evaluate an LLM's performance and response quality on short-text, conversational tasks.
